documents.title: amber-chords Documentation Index
documents.abstract: Entry point for the amber-chords RAG documentation set. Thirteen focused reference files totaling ~14k words, tuned for retrieval by AI coding agents building or extending the chord-corpus product.

documents.title: Music Theory Primer
documents.abstract: Terse reference for the harmonic concepts the amber-chords corpus encodes. Assumes familiarity with terms in the glossary. Pitch spelling follows key-signature convention unless stated otherwise.

documents.title: Chord Vocabulary
documents.abstract: Canonical chord-symbol set for the amber-chords corpus. Roughly 200 symbols, HookTheory-compatible, closed over 12 chromatic roots. All ingestion output is normalized to this enum; queries and ML features assume canonical form.

documents.title: Roman Numeral Derivation Spec
documents.abstract: Canonical grammar for Roman numerals used in the amber-chords corpus. Every chord symbol ingested from MusicXML is reduced to a Roman numeral token relative to a key. Defines the token set, case conventions, ambiguity resolution rules, and worked examples.

documents.title: MusicXML Reference for Chord Extraction
documents.abstract: Structure-of-a-MusicXML-file cheat sheet scoped to what the amber-chords ingestion pipeline needs: the element tree, the harmony payload, the kind enum, and the Sibelius export quirks that break naive parsers.

documents.title: music21 Ingestion Patterns
documents.abstract: Tight recipe file for the Python side of the amber-chords ingestion pipeline. Only the music21 APIs actually used in production are documented here. Pinned version: music21 >= 9.1. All snippets are complete and runnable.

documents.title: Schema Reference
documents.abstract: Canonical JSON shape for a chord-annotated song in amber-chords. Every persisted song, every API response, and every ingestion output conforms to this schema. TypeScript types are published as @amberaudio/chord-schema.

documents.title: Postgres Recipes
documents.abstract: Query cookbook for the songs table on Neon Postgres. All recipes assume the canonical schema with chord_json JSONB plus denormalized abs_sequence, rn_sequence, and chord_vocab arrays for index-friendly querying.

documents.title: Ingestion Pipeline
documents.abstract: End-to-end walkthrough of getting a chord-annotated song from Sibelius into the amber-chords Postgres database. Ten stages; any failure routes to a review queue with enough payload to replay. Pipeline is idempotent keyed on sha256 of raw MusicXML bytes.

documents.title: API Examples
documents.abstract: Request/response gallery for the amber-chords public API. Base URL https://chords.amberaudio.com/v1. All requests require bearer auth. Examples span jazz standards, Cantopop, modal pop, worship, and blues.

documents.title: Error Catalog
documents.abstract: Exhaustive list of failure modes emitted by the ingestion pipeline and API. Codes are stable and machine-readable; each row prescribes a recovery playbook. Severity drives alert routing.

documents.title: Testing Fixtures
documents.abstract: Catalog of MusicXML fixtures used by the ingest test suite. Each fixture exercises a specific branch of the parse to derivation to schema pipeline. Fixtures live under tests/fixtures/ with pinned expected.json siblings.

documents.title: ML Dataset Guide
documents.abstract: How the amber-chords corpus becomes training data. Covers the parquet export schema, tokenization choices, train/val/test splits, and dataset versioning. Ends with three concrete ML experiments the dataset is shaped to support.

documents.title: Glossary
documents.abstract: Alphabetical reference for terms used across the amber-chords corpus, ingestion pipeline, and API. Music-theory and engineering terms are interleaved. Definitions are one-sentence; follow cross-refs for depth.
